this_file_does_not_authorize: deletion of any object or function

respected_facts: payload_json has DB consumers (cg_core.v_cg_evento_scope, cg_core.v_cg_evidencia_unificada, multiple cg_mart audit views) and cannot be retired (Codex DISPUTED LOADER-FACT-003 / REJECTED R08).
cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows is a live dependency of cg_core.v_rr_frecuencia_base_resuelta_v2 and cg_mart.v_cg_ruta_duplicados_auditoria_v2 (Codex PARTIAL RUTA-INF-001).
row_hash is an analysis fingerprint, not the UPSERT key; UPSERT key is (source, source_row) (Codex VALIDATED RUTA-FACT-001).
9C7A4 functional daily/weekly mart parity is NOT closed: local daily recompute 34,817 vs baseline 21,348; weekly materialization timed out.

DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx cannot be retired with current DB coverage: public.fact_stock_venta covers 2026-05-02..2026-06-07; Hoja1 covers 2026-03-03..2026-04-06 (Codex REJECTED COMPLEXITY-HYP-001 / R10).
duplicados is redundant for the loader (Codex VALIDATED INVDATA-FACT-003) but its removal from the workbook is an operational decision for Bastian (R01).

COD_RT and NOMBRE_LOCAL_RR require a parity test before any offload, and the first parity source must be the same workbook RUTA_RUTERO sheet to avoid temporal mismatch (Codex PARTIAL INVDATA-HYP-001 / R07 REQUIRES_PARITY_TEST).

corrected_facts_from_codex.db_historica_internal: 10-column structure (Tabla4), 244,008 data rows, NOT BASE-identical schema
corrected_facts_from_codex.refresh_transaction_boundary: data mutation commits atomically BEFORE a separate post-commit best-effort ANALYZE; ANALYZE failure does not leave the mart in a partially-updated data state

programs.name: DATA PREPARATION OFFLOAD
programs.summary: Progressively replace Excel formula-derived columns with Python computation, proven by read-only parity before any productive loader change.

offload_order.reference_table: workbook RUTA_RUTERO sheet (same file, same period)

offload_order.priority: LOW; hit-rate unproven per Codex, do not build a DB table from current evidence
SEPARATE OPERATIONAL DECISION (Bastian), not a code offload

entradas: BASE sheet rows (43,635) from data/DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.xlsx: CADENA, FECHA, MARCA, SKU, DESCRIPCION_PRODUCTO, N_LOCAL, VTA(Un), INV(Un)
RUTA_RUTERO sheet (3,568 rows) from the SAME workbook: CADENA, COD B2B, CLIENTE, COD KPI ONE, LOCAL (workbook-internal to avoid temporal mismatch with live public.ruta_rutero)
outputs: Python-computed COD_RT per BASE row (resolved COD KPI ONE or NO_RT sentinel)
Python-computed NOMBRE_LOCAL_RR per BASE row (LOCAL name or NO_RT sentinel)
Parity report CSV/JSON comparing Python-computed vs Excel-cached values, never written into the productive pipeline

llaves.cod_rt_match: CADENA + N_LOCAL(=COD B2B) + MARCA(=CLIENTE) -> COD KPI ONE
llaves.fact_logical_key: (fecha, cod_rt, sku, marca) -- the DB UPSERT key used for row-by-row alignment

normalizaciones: Trim and upper-case key string columns to mirror Excel implicit coercion
N_LOCAL integer vs COD B2B type alignment (explicit cast; Excel XLOOKUP coerces implicitly)
SKU normalization (strip trailing .0) consistent with existing to_sku_text in loader
cod_rt upper-case consistent with existing loader str.upper()
conflictos: RUTA_RUTERO duplicate COD KPI ONE: Excel VLOOKUP takes first occurrence; Python must replicate first-match, not last-match
NO_RT sentinel rows: loader currently drops them silently; parity must count NO_RT identically
Multi-row matches in the XLOOKUP boolean product must resolve to the same single value Excel returns
design.comparacion_fila_a_fila: Join Python-computed frame and Excel-cached frame on (fecha, cod_rt_excel, sku, marca) plus row index; classify each row as MATCH, MISMATCH_VALUE, MISSING_PYTHON, EXTRA_PYTHON; export full diff. Use pandas assert_frame_equal on the logical key after alignment.
metricas_de_paridad: exact_match_rate on COD_RT (target 100% on a settled historical week)
row count equality and zero unmatched rows
NOMBRE_LOCAL_RR exact match rate conditional on COD_RT match
design.rollback: Prototype lives in a NEW standalone script (e.g. research/labs/), never edits scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py. Rollback = delete the lab script; productive loader is untouched at all times.
criterio_para_modificar_loader_productiv: COD_RT exact parity = 100% on at least one fully settled historical week using workbook RUTA_RUTERO
Codex independent re-run of the same parity reproduces the result
Explicit Bastian approval; only then design a loader PR with a feature flag and fallback to Excel-cached values

programs.objetivo: Eliminate the silent stale-formula-value risk for the most business-critical derived column (COD_RT) by computing it in Python from a temporally-aligned reference, proven by parity before touching production.
programs.beneficio_operacional: Removes the most error-prone manual dependency (formula recalculation/freshness) for route attribution; reduces silent CG metric corruption risk.
programs.beneficio_tecnico: Makes COD_RT lineage explicit and testable in code instead of opaque Excel array formulas (~155M comparisons per recalc).
programs.riesgo: MEDIUM. Type coercion and duplicate-key semantics can diverge from Excel; mitigated by using same-workbook RUTA_RUTERO and parity gating.
programs.costo: LOW-MEDIUM. One standalone parity script + one settled-week dataset; no DB, no productive change.
programs.reversibilidad: HIGH. Lab-only; nothing in the productive path changes until a separately-approved PR.
evidencia_disponible: CLAUDE-C001-INVDATA-FACT-002 (VALIDATED): four formula columns, loader reads saved values
CLAUDE-C001-INVDATA-FACT-004 (VALIDATED): COD_RT XLOOKUP over RUTA_RUTERO, stale-value risk
Codex R07 REQUIRES_PARITY_TEST with same-workbook RUTA_RUTERO first
evidencia_faltante: Whether RUTA_RUTERO has duplicate COD KPI ONE entries affecting first-match
Exact N_LOCAL vs COD B2B type/format alignment
A fully settled historical week to use as parity ground truth
programs.primer_experimento: Read-only COD_RT parity harness: compute COD_RT in Python from workbook RUTA_RUTERO, compare row-by-row against Excel-cached COD_RT on one settled week, emit parity metrics. No loader edit.
programs.criterio_de_exito: 100% COD_RT exact parity + equal NO_RT count + equal distribution on the settled week, reproduced independently by Codex.
programs.criterio_de_abandono: If parity cannot reach ~100% due to irreducible Excel coercion/duplicate semantics, abandon offload and instead keep COD_RT in Excel but add a freshness guard (reformulated R02).
dependencias: No dependency on DB or on Program B

programs.name: SUPABASE STORAGE AND PARITY
programs.summary: A strictly gated path that resolves functional mart parity FIRST, then catalogs DB objects, then measures archivable raw, never retiring payload_json without an equivalent surface.

strict_order_gates.name: Functional daily/weekly parity
strict_order_gates.exit_criteria: Local recompute of cg_mart.fact_cg_visita_dia_resuelta_v2 and v_cg_out_weekly_v2 matches baseline exactly (close the 9C7A4 +13,469 daily surplus and weekly timeout).

strict_order_gates.name: Authoritative DB object + view-dependency catalog
strict_order_gates.exit_criteria: Complete catalog of all schemas/objects with column-level payload_json and row_hash consumers; supersedes the partial 28-object estimate.

strict_order_gates.name: Raw classification
strict_order_gates.exit_criteria: Every raw row classified as raw_necesario | raw_reconstruible | raw_historico with the batch-window selector (v_cg_latest_batch_by_source) as the boundary.

strict_order_gates.name: Retention/archival design (no execution)
strict_order_gates.exit_criteria: Reconstructability-proven archival plan for raw_reconstruible and raw_historico, with rollback rehearsed; payload_json retained or replaced by a proven-equivalent surface.

raw_taxonomy.raw_necesario: Batches currently selected by v_cg_latest_batch_by_source and consumed by the v2 mart pipeline; must remain online.
raw_taxonomy.raw_reconstruible: Older batches reproducible from validated local archives (9C7A3 parquet exports, hashes verified); candidate for cold archive only after G0+G2.
raw_taxonomy.raw_historico: Batches with no reconstruction need and outside any reporting window; candidate for archival after Bastian retention policy.

never_do: Never retire payload_json without a proven-equivalent surface (REJECTED R08); note SP Item ID exists ONLY in payload_json for KPIONE2.
Never apply arbitrary retention windows (12 weeks / 52 loads) before G2 measurement (REFORMULATE R04/R05).
Never delete raw before functional parity (G0) proves the mart is reproducible.
programs.objetivo: Bring Supabase storage under control (currently ~1.43 GB vs 512 MB free target) safely, without breaking the v2 mart that reads raw via DB views.
programs.beneficio_operacional: Avoids hitting the free-plan storage limit and reduces cost; protects CG reporting integrity.
programs.beneficio_tecnico: Produces the authoritative DB catalog the project lacks and a reconstructability contract for raw.
programs.riesgo: HIGH if attempted before G0; data loss / mart corruption. LOW if gates are honored.
programs.costo: HIGH. Requires Codex DB work, parity root-cause, and rehearsed rollback.
programs.reversibilidad: MEDIUM-LOW for any archival step; HIGH for the measurement/catalog steps (read-only).
evidencia_disponible: 9C7A2 storage forensics: cg_raw ~1.43 GB, kpione2_raw 91% duplication, FULL_SNAPSHOT_ACCUMULATIVE overlap
9C7A4 dependency graph + DDL: payload_json and ruta_rutero_load_rows consumers confirmed
9C7A3 validated parquet archive (71 files, hashes OK) as reconstruction source
evidencia_faltante: Root cause of 9C7A4 daily +13,469 surplus (batch-window selection semantics)
MIN(fecha) authoritative coverage of fact_stock_venta vs external archive
Per-source list of payload-only keys beyond KPIONE2 SP Item ID
programs.primer_experimento: (Codex-owned) Root-cause the daily surplus: compare local recompute vs baseline keys to isolate whether extra rows come from out-of-window raw batches or from latest-batch selection logic.
programs.criterio_de_exito: G0 reached: exact daily AND weekly parity, reproduced.
programs.criterio_de_abandono: If functional parity cannot be reproduced locally, escalate to a controlled live-snapshot diagnostic; do not proceed to any archival design.
dependencias: Partially informed by Program C catalog work
programs.prioridad: P3 (must wait; Gate G0 is the prerequisite and is owned by Codex)

programs.name: CODE SIMPLIFICATION
programs.summary: Reduce app/db.py risk through observability and verified-dead-code confirmation, with NO big rewrite and NO removal of the B3 fallback in stage one.

stages.name: Runtime smoke harness
stages.action: Build a runtime smoke that exercises each screen path (CLIENTE, CONTROL GESTION v2, LOCAL, MERCADERISTA) and records which db.* functions actually execute.

stages.name: Confirm static-only functions are runtime-dead
stages.action: Cross-check the 10 Codex-validated static-only functions (plus get_rutero_reponedor) against the smoke trace; mark as runtime-confirmed-dead only if never invoked.

stages.name: Make B3 fallback visible
stages.action: Add observability (structured log/metric) when the B3 legacy path is reached via exception fallthrough or USE_CG_V2!=1; do NOT remove B3.
stages.constraint: B3 removal is explicitly out of scope for stage one.

stages.name: Responsibility separation (design only)
stages.action: Propose a seam-based extraction plan for app/db.py (infra / selectors / CG-v2 / coverage-audit) without moving code yet; sequence small, independently-revertable PRs.

stages.name: Approved cleanup PRs (gated)
stages.action: Only after C2 runtime confirmation + Bastian approval, design deletion PRs for runtime-confirmed-dead functions, each with smoke before/after.

programs.objetivo: Lower the maintenance risk of the 5,348-line app/db.py and surface the B3 fallback, without a risky rewrite.
programs.beneficio_operacional: Fewer latent failure modes; B3 activation becomes observable instead of silent.
programs.beneficio_tecnico: Verified dead-code list and a safe, incremental decomposition path; runtime smoke becomes reusable regression infrastructure.
programs.riesgo: LOW for C1-C4 (additive/observability); MEDIUM for C5 (deletion) gated behind smoke + approval.
programs.costo: MEDIUM. Building a runtime smoke harness is the main effort.
programs.reversibilidad: HIGH for C1-C4; C5 deletions are revertable via git and guarded by before/after smoke.
evidencia_disponible: CLAUDE-C001-DBPY-FACT-001 (VALIDATED): 10 functions static-only, no dynamic dispatch
CLAUDE-C001-DBPY-FACT-003 (VALIDATED): Home.py defers imports inside main()
CG-001 (VALIDATED): B3 has active callers and exception-fallthrough path
evidencia_faltante: Runtime trace proving the static-only functions are never invoked dynamically at runtime
Whether the static-only functions are abandoned vs intentionally-diagnostic (Codex PARTIAL DBPY-INF-001)
programs.primer_experimento: Stand up the runtime smoke harness (C1) and capture a db.* invocation trace across all four screens.
programs.criterio_de_exito: Smoke covers all screen paths and yields a reproducible db.* invocation set; static-only set confirmed absent from it.
programs.criterio_de_abandono: If a 'static-only' function appears in the runtime trace, immediately reclassify it as live and stop any cleanup consideration for it.
dependencias: Independent of Program A and B
C5 depends on C2 + Bastian approval

recommendation.first_program_reason: Highest validated evidence (INVDATA-FACT-002/004 VALIDATED), read-only and lab-isolated, uses same-workbook RUTA_RUTERO so no DB and no temporal mismatch, fully reversible, and directly attacks the top silent-error risk (stale COD_RT). Matches Codex recommended order item 2.
recommendation.parallel_program_reason: Independent of A and of the DB; C1-C4 are additive/observability with low risk. Building the runtime smoke and confirming the validated static-only set can proceed without blocking on parity.
recommendation.deferred_program_reason: Gate G0 (functional daily/weekly parity) is open and BLOCKED by the 9C7A4 failure (34,817 vs 21,348 daily; weekly timeout). No retention/archival or payload_json work is safe until G0 closes, and payload_json removal is REJECTED outright.
do_not_execute: Do NOT remove payload_json from any cg_raw or cg_core table (REJECTED R08; SP Item ID is payload-only for KPIONE2).
Do NOT retire DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx or the internal DB_HISTORICA sheet (REJECTED_FOR_NOW R10; DB does not cover 2026-03-03..2026-04-06).
Do NOT delete any static-only app/db.py function yet (requires runtime smoke confirmation + Bastian approval).
Do NOT remove or bypass the B3 fallback in stage one.
Do NOT modify scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py or any productive loader before COD_RT parity is proven and approved.
Do NOT apply arbitrary retention windows (12 weeks / 52 loads) to cg_raw or ruta history before G2 measurement.
Do NOT use live public.ruta_rutero for the first COD_RT parity test (temporal mismatch); use the workbook RUTA_RUTERO sheet.
Do NOT remove the workbook duplicados column without Bastian operational confirmation (R01).
Do NOT build a FOCO_CLIENTE DB table for OTROS from current evidence (hit-rate unproven).
Do NOT delete or apply sql/09 and sql/10 drafts based on filename/git status alone.

codex_future_phases.title: Read-only COD_RT parity harness from workbook RUTA_RUTERO
codex_future_phases.gate: produces the parity evidence required by R07

codex_future_phases.title: Root-cause and close 9C7A4 daily/weekly functional mart parity
Runtime smoke harness + confirm static-only functions are runtime-dead
codex_future_phases.gate: produces runtime confirmation for any future cleanup

codex_future_phases.title: Authoritative DB object and view-dependency catalog with column-level payload_json/row_hash consumers
Bastian decision pack: duplicados removal, authoritative historical source, manual CG sheets (DESARROLLO/OUT), retention policy

non_authority_notice: This portfolio synthesizes validated research into a prioritized plan. It authorizes no runtime, loader, SQL, DB, data, or evidence changes. Every program step remains gated behind explicit Bastian approval and, where stated, Codex parity/catalog evidence.
